hero.title: Building the Future of AI-Powered Development
hero.subtitle: We're on a mission to enable seamless collaboration between humans and AI coding agents, transforming how software is built.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.content: AgentsMesh was founded with a simple belief: the future of software development lies in the collaboration between humans and AI agents. We're building the infrastructure that enables multiple AI coding agents to work together, coordinated by human developers, to tackle complex development challenges at unprecedented speed and scale.

autonomous.title: Autonomous Execution
autonomous.content: AI agents should be able to independently complete complex tasks, not just assist with code completion.

collaboration.title: Multi-Agent Collaboration
collaboration.content: The most complex problems require multiple specialized agents working together seamlessly.

security.title: Data Sovereignty
security.content: Your code and data should never leave your infrastructure without your explicit consent.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.description: AgentsMesh is a Terminal-based Coding Agent collaboration platform that enables teams to leverage AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and Aider at scale. Unlike IDE plugins (Cursor, Copilot), AgentsMesh focuses on autonomous terminal agents with full system access, enabling multi-agent coordination (AgentsMesh), remote AI workstations (AgentPod), and integrated task management.

whyTerminal.title: Why Terminal-based Agents?
whyTerminal.description: Terminal-based coding agents offer significant advantages over IDE plugins:

quickLinks.title: Quick Links
quickLinks.quickStartDesc: Get up and running in 5 minutes

hero.title: Blog
hero.subtitle: Technical insights, product updates, and stories from the AgentsMesh team.

introducing.title: Introducing AgentsMesh: The Future of AI-Powered Development
introducing.excerpt: Today we're excited to announce AgentsMesh, a platform that enables multiple AI coding agents to collaborate on complex development tasks.
introducing.content:
Today we're excited to announce AgentsMesh, a platform that enables multiple AI coding agents to collaborate on complex development tasks.

The way we build software is changing. AI coding agents like Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Aider are becoming increasingly capable of handling complex development tasks autonomously. But as these agents become more powerful, a new challenge emerges: how do we coordinate multiple agents working on the same project?

AgentsMesh solves this challenge by providing:

• AgentPod: Remote AI workstations that run agents in isolated environments
• AgentsMesh Channels: Communication infrastructure for multi-agent collaboration
• Self-Hosted Runners: Keep your code and data in your own infrastructure

We believe the future of software development lies in human-AI collaboration, where developers orchestrate teams of AI agents to tackle complex challenges at unprecedented speed and scale.

Get started for free at agentsmesh.dev

multiAgent.title: Understanding Multi-Agent Collaboration in Software Development
multiAgent.excerpt: Explore how multiple AI agents can work together to solve complex development challenges more effectively than single-agent approaches.
multiAgent.content:
As AI coding agents become more sophisticated, we're discovering that some problems are best solved by multiple specialized agents working together.

Consider a typical feature implementation: it might require understanding existing code, designing new components, writing tests, and updating documentation. Each of these tasks benefits from different approaches and contexts.

With AgentsMesh, you can:

• Assign different agents to different aspects of a task
• Enable agents to communicate through channels
• Coordinate work through pod bindings and permissions
• Visualize the collaboration topology in real-time

This approach mirrors how human development teams work, with specialized roles collaborating toward a common goal. The difference? AI agents can work continuously, share context instantly, and iterate at machine speed.

Learn more about multi-agent collaboration in our documentation.

selfHosted.title: Why Self-Hosted Runners Matter for Enterprise AI Development
selfHosted.excerpt: For enterprises with strict security and compliance requirements, self-hosted runners provide the control and data sovereignty they need.
selfHosted.content:
When adopting AI development tools, enterprises face a critical question: where does the code go?

With AgentsMesh's self-hosted runners, the answer is simple: your code stays in your infrastructure. Here's why this matters:

• Data Sovereignty: Code never leaves your network
• Compliance: Meet regulatory requirements for data handling
• Security: Full control over access and audit logs
• Performance: Optimized for your specific environment

Deploying a runner is straightforward:

1. Generate a registration token in your AgentsMesh dashboard
2. Run the runner binary on your infrastructure
3. Start creating pods that execute in your environment

Whether you're running on bare metal, VMs, or Kubernetes, AgentsMesh runners adapt to your infrastructure.

Contact us to learn more about enterprise deployment options.
